title: Dachshund-Shaped CD Holder
summary: A dachshund-shaped CD holder cut from a printable pattern, using bent metal flashing brackets to support the CD stack.
description:
This holder gets its whole personality from one silhouette — a long, low dachshund body that happens to be exactly the right shape to hold a stack of CDs between its legs. The novelty shape is the entire design; the actual support mechanism is just two bent metal brackets.

The brackets are cut from **sheet metal flashing, not wood** — a wooden support piece thick enough to hold the CD stack's weight would be visually clunky against the dog's thin legs, where thin bent metal disappears into the silhouette.

A 1in-grid printed pattern is what gets a design this specific onto the wood accurately — freehand-sketching a recognizable dog silhouette at this small a scale is a lot harder than tracing a grid-transferred template.

materials.name: Bent metal flashing, 3"
materials.note: Support brackets — cut with tin snips.

steps.title: Transfer the pattern
steps.body: Print the body pattern in landscape mode at 100% (grid squares should be 1in) and transfer it to the 3/4in pine.

steps.title: Cut the body pieces
steps.body: Cut two each of the front and back leg shapes and two body/back pieces, then sand all pieces before gluing them together.

steps.title: Cut and shape the brackets
steps.body: Cut two 3in lengths of metal flashing for support brackets, filing the edges and rounding the corners.

steps.title: Mount the brackets
steps.body: Drill mounting holes in the brackets and dimple the back pieces to match, then fasten the brackets in place with wood screws.
